understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to complete all levels by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile simultaneously. In the level I attempted, there were two selectable pieces (P and S) and two goal tiles ('+') in the semantic grid, so both pieces needed to be on the goals at the same time to win.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement shifts the selected piece in grid-aligned steps of 3 pixels per cell. Heuristic movement_blocked hints often indicate adjacent objects but do not guarantee a move will fail—walls or blocks can sometimes be pushed or navigated around. Pushable objects (blocks) move along with the selected piece when pushed. Walls can block movement, but their behavior can vary (some boundaries might be permeable or require specific interaction to pass). Switching control between multiple selectable pieces is done via CLICK with a target_position in the same pixel/grid coordinates as shown in the state dict. The level required both pieces to be on goals simultaneously to trigger the WIN condition.
